title: Temple of Waru
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''Temple of Waru''' was a structure that housed [[Waru]], a being from another dimension. Waru arrived in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] due to a combination of the disturbances in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] from a crystallizing [[Star/Legends|star]], and [[Hethrir]]'s [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] of his prisoners, many of whom Hethrir sacrificed to Waru in the temple. Hethrir came to Waru in hopes of mastering the Force and as a reward he would sacrifice young [[Anakin Solo]] in the temple. However, Hethrir was destroyed during a confrontation with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].

== Description ==
The temple of [[Waru]] was located on [[Crseih Station]] in the [[Crseih system/Legends|Crseih system]]. It was constructed at the center of a large crater that was encased in a translucent green dome. Ruins and large stones lined the edges of the crater, with several rough paths leading down to a small complex of builds which contained the temple compound.<ref name=":0">''[[The Crystal Star]]''</ref>

The complex's buildings were gilt and brightly lit. Calligraphy, esoteric hieroglyphs, and intricate designs were traced in gold on the buildings' walls and entry facade. Wings of the temple curved around to form a quiet courtyard. Inside the temple was a wide, low theater where an [[Altar of Waru|altar]] holding Waru loomed over the auditorium.<ref name=":0" />

== History ==
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Crseih Station was moved to the Crseih system where it orbited a [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]] and a crystallizing white dwarf star. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] used this station as a research station for experiments that tested the limits of [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]], [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and [[otherspace]].<ref name=":0" /> Following the death of the [[Cloning/Legends|reborn]] [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]] of the [[Empire Reborn]] took over the station to find a way to expand his powers and extend his life.<ref name=":1">''[[Aliens in the Empire]]''</ref> The combination of his scientists' experimentation, his rituals, and the disturbances in the [[The Force/Legends|Force]] from the crystal star created a space-time distortion that yanked the extradimensional entity named Waru into the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Hethrir made a deal to feed Waru [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]] so he could attain the power to open a vortex to his home dimension. In exchange, Waru was to complete Hethrir's [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] transformation.<ref name=":0" />

A temple to Waru was built and Crseih Station was renamed to Asylum Station. News of Waru's healing abilities spread and attracted desperate pilgrims from across the galaxy. These pilgrims filled the aisles, seats, and resting pillows in the temple auditorium as they waited for Waru to heal them or their loved ones. Waru used a portion of his [[anti-Force]] powers to heal his followers, and replaced the energy he expended by consuming the life force of others. Waru made it appear that his failure to heal some were either accidents, or because the person he healed did not believe in him enough.<ref name=":0" /> 

Hethrir collected Force-sensitive youths from multiple backwater worlds to create a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Reborn|army]] for his Empire Reborn. He brought his [[Empire Youth]] trainees to Waru's temple to be "purified". Those who survived were passed on to the Dark Jedi [[Desann]] for training, but many of the children were consumed by Waru.<ref name=":1" /> 

Around [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], Hethrir had abducted [[Anakin Solo]] and brought him to the temple to feed Waru. He was saved by his parents, and Waru attempted to consume [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] instead. When this meal was denied to him, Waru turned on Hethrir and consumed him, gaining enough energy to return to his dimension. With Waru gone, his temple was likely abandoned after Crseih Station was moved to the [[Pakuuni system/Legends|Pakuuni system]].<ref name=":0" />   

==Appearances==
*''[[The Crystal Star]]'' {{1st}}
